name: Hermitage Virgin Sierra de Arcos from Ariño (GR 262)
name_original: Ermita Virgen Sierra de Arcos desde Ariño (GR 262)
region_intro: Hiking around Ariño, located in the Río Martín Valley in Teruel, Aragon, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Arcos mountain range, providing a scenic backdrop and varied topography. The Martín River flows through the area, creating lush environments and riverside paths, including the Cueva Foradada reservoir. These natural features contribute to a rich hydrological landscape with numerous hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Ariño The most popular hiking route is Hermitage Virgin Sierra de Arcos from Ariño (GR 262), a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route highlights the natural beauty and offers a glimpse of historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Oliete and the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal Loop, a moderate 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This trail visits the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal and the scenic La Portillada gorge. Local hikers also love the Cave of the Engravings – Los Chaparros Rock Shelter loop from Azud los Estrechos, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Ariño is defined by the Sierra de Arcos, the Martín River, and its valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Ariño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Ariño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Ariño?
A: Ariño is situated at the foot of the Sierra de Arcos, offering varied topography and scenic mountain views. The Martín River flows through the region, creating lush riverside environments and contributing to a rich hydrological landscape, including the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Ariño?
A: Yes, Ariño offers several easy routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Silleta loop from PR-TE 119&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7 km hike with rewarding views. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Engravings – Los Chaparros Rock Shelter loop from Casas de los Bataneros&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.17 km long and also rated easy.

Q: What are some notable historical or cultural sites I can visit while hiking?
A: The region is rich in history. You can incorporate a visit to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Arcos&lt;/a&gt;, a historical-artistic monument located a few kilometers from Ariño. Some trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979745327/hermitage-virgin-sierra-de-arcos-from-arino-gr-262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage Virgin Sierra de Arcos from Ariño (GR 262)&lt;/a&gt;, also offer glimpses of historical sites along the way.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ariño?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979637651/oliete-and-the-hermitage-of-the-virgin-of-cantal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oliete and the Hermitage of the Virgin of Cantal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4 km circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31704565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Engravings – Los Chaparros Rock Shelter loop from Azud los Estrechos&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.2 km loop combining natural exploration with historical interest.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ariño?
A: The hiking routes in Ariño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the natural beauty of the Sierra de Arcos, and the peaceful riverside paths along the Martín River.

Q: Are there any trails that feature water bodies or opportunities for wild swimming?
A: The Martín River flows through the region, offering beautiful riverside walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3618839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Foradada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, located on the Martín River near Oliete, is a tranquil spot perfect for enjoying the water, especially on warm days.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Ariño?
A: Ariño offers a good mix of difficulty levels. Out of 11 available routes, 4 are rated as easy, and 7 are considered moderate. There are no difficult trails, making the area accessible for a wide range of hikers.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic views on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, many trails in Ariño offer rewarding views due to the varied topography of the Sierra de Arcos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Silleta loop from PR-TE 119&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for its views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alloza and the Church of the Purísima Concepción&lt;/a&gt; is a designated viewpoint in the area.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Ariño?
A: The diverse landscapes of Ariño, with its rivers and mountains, make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but riverside walks and spots like the Cueva Foradada Reservoir provide opportunities to cool down.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging moderate hikes available?
A: For those looking for a longer moderate hike, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979745327/hermitage-virgin-sierra-de-arcos-from-arino-gr-262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage Virgin Sierra de Arcos from Ariño (GR 262)&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.7 km route that takes approximately 3 hours 30 minutes to complete, offering a good challenge with natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: What cultural experiences can I combine with hiking in the Ariño region?
A: Ariño is part of the Ruta del Tambor y el Bombo, a famous route through nine villages in the Bajo Martín region known for their Holy Week drum processions, which are recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO. This offers a unique blend of cultural immersion and walking opportunities.
tips: The Hermitage is located a few kilometers from the town of Ariño and since 1983 it has been a historical-artistic monument. Also popularly known as a monastery, the function in its beginnings was the exercise of political and religious power over the neighboring towns, sometimes even over an entire region. Today many are in ruins but can be visited to understand a little more how they functioned in a time very different from today.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of Arcos
